title: stripeの接続エラーが出ている。至急修正せよ。
summary_text: Concrete pitfalls to address before calling the plan “sound”: - Use a brand-new disposable email every run; reusing an old user can hide regressions (existing `stripe_customer_id` path instead of creation/self-heal path). - Run `checkout` first, then `portal`; if `portal` is first and customer creation regressed there, you can get a false negative unrelated to checkout. - Validate returned URLs by host (`checkout.stripe.com` / `billing.stripe.com`), not just HTTP 200 + `url` field. - Record IDs before cleanup (`auth.users.id`, `ca_users.id`, `stripe_customer_id`); deleting auth first can make later targeted cleanup ambiguous. - Cleanup order should be: cancel/delete Stripe test customer objects first, then DB rows (`ca_subscriptions`/`ca_users` test rows), then `auth.users`. - Guard cleanup with a unique test marker (email prefix/metadata) so you never delete non-test production records. - After cleanup, verify zero leftovers for that marker in `auth.users`, `ca_users`, and Stripe customers; otherwise the run is not complete.
